title: 字母袖扣谋杀案
summary:
After being informed by Mr. Lazarus that a fourth murder has occurred at the Brawley's Hotel, Poirot and Cattermole prepare to depart for the Bluxton Hotel. Mrs. Brandonsworthy warns Cattermole to be cautious, noting that three people have been killed at the Brawnsham Hotel within the past week and suggesting that guests move to less luxurious accommodations to avoid being targeted.

During the car ride, Poirot expresses frustration at his failure to prevent the fourth murder. He questions Cattermole regarding the investigation at Grellingsholme, specifically concerning Jenny and Margaret Ernst. Poirot suspects that Margaret Ernst delayed sharing information about Patrick and Francis Yve because she wished to consult Dr. Ambrose Flavard, whom Cattermole admits she admired. Poirot argues that Margaret’s passion for the deceased Evans couple was actually a reflection of her love for Dr. Flavard. Cattermole provides new autopsy results, revealing that the three previous victims—Harriet Sipper, Ada Gransbury, and Richard Nix—all died of cyanide poisoning. Notably, none had food in their stomachs, suggesting their afternoon tea had disappeared. Poirot claims the mystery of the missing food is already solved.

Upon arriving at the Bluxton Hotel, they meet Henry Nigges, the younger brother of the victim Richard Nigges. Henry, who is unaware of the fourth murder, explains that he has been staying at the Langham Hotel and has come to collect Richard's belongings. He avoids looking at a suitcase labeled "Mr. R. Negus" before departing, requesting that the investigators keep him informed.
